title: Cyrillic Alphabet
description: Learn Bulgarian letters, the sounds they make, and the tricky ones to watch for.

content.title: How the Alphabet Works
content.description: Bulgarian uses Cyrillic. Most letters map consistently to sounds, but a few need extra attention.

content.title: Study tip
content.text: Read letters out loud in groups of vowels, consonants, and special sounds. That makes the alphabet easier to memorize.

content.title: What to notice first
items: Bulgarian has 30 letters in modern standard use.
Several letters look familiar but sound different from English.
The letter Ъ is a uniquely Bulgarian vowel sound.

content.title: Letters and Sounds
content.description: A full reference table for the Bulgarian alphabet.

content.title: Tricky Letters
content.description: These letters are easy to confuse at first, so they are worth practicing separately.

cards.hint: The long consonant cluster in Bulgarian words like 'щастие'.

content.note: The word 'ще' is one of the most common future-tense markers.

content.title: Practice Words
content.description: Short words are the fastest way to connect letters with sound and meaning.

rows: Ends with a strong k sound

content.question: Which letter is the uniquely Bulgarian vowel?
content.note: It often surprises English speakers because it does not have a direct equivalent.
